# SamplingFrame { #fmrimod.SamplingFrame }

```python
SamplingFrame(
    blocklens=None,
    tr=None,
    start_time=None,
    precision=0.1,
    *,
    n_scans=None,
    TR=None,
)
```

fMRI acquisition timing specification.

This class represents the temporal sampling structure of an fMRI experiment,
including the timing of acquisitions, block structure, and precision settings.

Attributes:
    blocklens: Number of scans per block (array or scalar)
    tr: Repetition time(s) in seconds (array or scalar)
    start_time: Start time offset(s) in seconds (array or scalar, default TR/2)
    precision: Temporal precision for convolution in seconds

## Attributes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [TR](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.TR) | Repetition time (scalar, for backward compatibility). |
| [acquisition_onsets](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.acquisition_onsets) | Get fMRI acquisition onset times. |
| [blockids](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.blockids) | Block ID for each scan. |
| [global_scan_times](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.global_scan_times) | Global scan times across all blocks. |
| [n_blocks](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.n_blocks) | Number of blocks. |
| [n_scans](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.n_scans) | Total number of scans across all blocks. |
| [samples](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.samples) | Time points for each scan. |

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [block_samples](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.block_samples) | Get time points for a specific block. |
| [concatenate](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.concatenate) | Concatenate two sampling frames. |
| [from_dict](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.from_dict) | Create SamplingFrame from dictionary. |
| [global_onsets](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.global_onsets) | Convert block-relative onset times to global (experiment-wide) onset times. |
| [sample_times](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.sample_times) | Get time points for scans with optional filtering. |
| [to_dict](#fmrimod.SamplingFrame.to_dict) | Convert to dictionary representation. |

### block_samples { #fmrimod.SamplingFrame.block_samples }

```python
SamplingFrame.block_samples(block_idx)
```

Get time points for a specific block.

Args:
    block_idx: Block index (0-based)

Returns:
    Array of time points for the specified block

### concatenate { #fmrimod.SamplingFrame.concatenate }

```python
SamplingFrame.concatenate(other)
```

Concatenate two sampling frames.

Args:
    other: Another SamplingFrame to concatenate

Returns:
    New SamplingFrame with combined blocks

### from_dict { #fmrimod.SamplingFrame.from_dict }

```python
SamplingFrame.from_dict(data)
```

Create SamplingFrame from dictionary.

Args:
    data: Dictionary with sampling frame parameters

Returns:
    New SamplingFrame instance

### global_onsets { #fmrimod.SamplingFrame.global_onsets }

```python
SamplingFrame.global_onsets(onsets, blockids)
```

Convert block-relative onset times to global (experiment-wide) onset times.

Args:
    onsets: Numeric array of onset times within blocks (block-relative).
    blockids: Integer array identifying which block each onset belongs to
        (0-based).

Returns:
    Array of global onset times.

Raises:
    ValueError: If onsets and blockids have different lengths or blockids are out of range.

### sample_times { #fmrimod.SamplingFrame.sample_times }

```python
SamplingFrame.sample_times(global_time=True, blockids=None)
```

Get time points for scans with optional filtering.

Args:
    global_time: If True, return global times; if False, return block-relative times
    blockids: Optional 0-based block IDs to filter.

Returns:
    Array of time points in seconds

### to_dict { #fmrimod.SamplingFrame.to_dict }

```python
SamplingFrame.to_dict()
```

Convert to dictionary representation.

Returns:
    Dictionary with all sampling frame parameters
